seo.title: Building Research Agents with Claude Tool Use + Vex Guardrails
seo.description: Learn how to build research agents with Claude Tool Use + Vex guardrails. Detect failures, prevent drift, and ensure reliability.

content.intro: Building Research Agents with Claude's native tool use enables multi-step web search, analysis, and synthesis for accurate information retrieval. However, tool chains often cascade errors from early failures, parallel calls yield conflicting data, and extended reasoning drifts from outputs, risking unreliable summaries. Vex guardrails verify tool responses, claims, sources, and bias to ensure trustworthy research agents.

architectureOverview.heading: Research Agents Architecture with Claude Tool Use
architectureOverview.description:
Research Agents using Claude Tool Use follow a multi-step loop: Claude receives a query, calls tools like web_search or browse_page in parallel or sequence, processes results with reasoning, and synthesizes a final summary. Tools return structured data (e.g., search snippets, page content) which Claude analyzes for recency, relevance, and accuracy before outputting claims with citations.

The agent maintains conversation state, using tool results to refine queries iteratively. Parallel tool calls gather diverse sources, but require resolution logic to handle conflicts. Computer use tools may scrape or interact with web elements, increasing risk of unintended actions.

Verification occurs at key checkpoints: after tool responses, during claim extraction, and before final synthesis, ensuring source validity, factual accuracy, and bias neutrality.
architectureOverview.diagram:
Query → Claude Agent
         ↓
Parallel Tool Calls
├── web_search(query)
├── browse_page(url)
└── get_page_metadata(url)
         ↓
Tool Results → Vex.verify()
         ↓
Claude Reasoning & Synthesis
         ↓
Final Summary + Citations

risks.name: Cascading Tool Failures
risks.description: Early web_search API errors propagate through analysis, producing hallucinated summaries from empty results
risks.mitigation: Wrap each tool_use response with vex.verify() to catch and retry failures immediately

risks.name: Conflicting Parallel Results
risks.description: Multiple search tools return contradictory facts without resolution, leading to inaccurate synthesis
risks.mitigation: Verify claim consistency across parallel tool outputs before Claude reasoning step

risks.name: Reasoning Drift
risks.description: Extended thinking traces contradict final output claims, especially in complex multi-source analysis
risks.mitigation: Compare reasoning chain with final claims using Vex semantic verification

risks.name: Outdated Source Usage
risks.description: Agent cites stale web results without recency checks, delivering obsolete research findings
risks.mitigation: Enforce recency verification on all source metadata during tool result processing

vexIntegration.title: Verify Tool Use Responses
vexIntegration.description: Wrap Claude tool_use messages with Vex to catch cascading errors and validate sources

vexIntegration.title: Claim Accuracy & Bias Check
vexIntegration.description: Verify final research synthesis for factual accuracy and neutrality
vexIntegration.code:
final_response = client.messages.create(
    model="claude-3-5-sonnet-20240620",
    messages=messages
)

# Extract claims and verify
claims = extract_claims(final_response.content[0].text)

verification = vex.verify({
    "claims": claims,
    "sources": cited_sources,
    "checks": [
        "claim_accuracy",
        "bias_detection",
        "recency_checks",
        "source_verification"
    ],
    "policy": "medium"
})

if verification.status == "pass":
    print("✅ Research verified:", verification.summary)
else:
    print("❌ Issues found:", verification.issues)
    # Trigger human review or retry
